summary: Schema-impacting package: treat migrations as production-critical. Default to additive changes, staged nullability, and measurable backfills.
backwardsCompatibility: Prefer additive columns and nullable rollout phases before tightening NOT NULL constraints.
Avoid renaming columns without a compatibility view or dual-write period unless explicitly authorized.
nullabilityRisks: Tightening nullability can fail on existing rows; plan staged backfills and validation queries.
New required foreign keys may orphan historical rows; assess retention and archival paths.
indexPerformance: New filters/sorts may require composite indexes; verify explain plans for list endpoints.
Large backfills can lock tables; batch updates and consider off-hours maintenance windows.
seedBackfill: Identify minimal seed rows required for dev/stage parity; avoid auto-seeding production business data from generators.
Document any required manual backfill scripts and verification queries.
auditRetention: Do not rewrite immutable audit history; append-only patterns preserve traceability.
If migrating historical records, preserve original timestamps in dedicated columns where feasible.
trainingDocumentRisks: Schema changes to document/training join tables can break effective-date logic; verify revision coupling.
If workflow states rename, ensure training assignments still map to correct operational states.
rolloutCautions: Ship behind feature flags or staged rollout if workflow and schema change together.
Coordinate cache invalidation and API versioning if mobile/integrations consume the same endpoints.
destructiveWarnings: DROP/TRUNCATE/CASCADE deletes are high risk; require explicit sign-off, backups, and restore drill.
Data migrations that reinterpret meaning (e.g., status remaps) must include reconciliation reports.
workflowNotes: Workflow snapshot includes 6 states; align schema with transition persistence.
